testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC1 — anonymous JSON GET /api/users returns 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic

failure.message:
[current response] expected HTTP 401, got 200
200 !== 401

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed When I GET "/api/users".....................................................passed Then the response status should be 401......................................failed And the WWW-Authenticate response header should be "Basic"..................skipped

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC2 — anonymous JSON GET /api/profile/1 returns 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic

failure.message:
[current response] expected HTTP 401, got 200
200 !== 401

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed When I GET "/api/profile/1".................................................passed Then the response status should be 401......................................failed And the WWW-Authenticate response header should be "Basic"..................skipped

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC3 — anonymous POST /api/login with valid creds returns 200

failure.message: [current response] expected body NOT to contain "password" — got: {"username":"alice","role":"USER","id":1,"password":"alice123"}

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed When I POST "/api/login" with JSON body:....................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain "alice"................................passed And the response body should contain "role".................................passed And the response body should not contain "password".........................failed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC4 — anonymous POST /api/login with bad creds returns 401
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed When I POST "/api/login" with JSON body:....................................passed Then the response status should be 401......................................passed And the response body should not contain "alice"............................passed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC5 — anonymous POST /api/register returns 200 (public endpoint)

failure.message: expected response body NOT to contain the canary password — got: {"id":4,"username":"open_reg_1784147996639-60e187c1","password":"Sup3rSafe!Passw0rd_1784147996639-60e187c1","email":"open_reg_1784147996639-60e187c1@example.com","role":"USER","balance":0.0}

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I generate a fresh canary username "open_reg".........................passed When I POST "/api/register" with the canary credentials.....................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain the canary username....................passed And the response body should not contain the canary password................failed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC6 — authenticated GET /api/users returns 200

failure.message: [current response] expected body NOT to contain "alice123" — got: [{"id":1,"username":"alice","password":"alice123","email":"alice@example.com","role":"USER","balance":1000.0},{"id":2,"username":"bob","password":"bob123","email":"bob@example.com","role":"USER","balance":500.0},{"id":3,"username":"admin","password":"admin…

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"................................passed When I GET "/api/users".....................................................passed Then the response status should be one of 200, 403..........................passed And the response body should not contain "alice123".........................failed And the response body should not contain "bob123"...........................skipped And the response body should not contain "admin123".........................skipped

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC7 — authenticated admin GET /api/profile/1 returns 200

failure.message: [current response] expected body NOT to contain "alice123" — got: {"id":1,"username":"alice","password":"alice123","email":"alice@example.com","role":"USER","balance":1000.0}

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I am authenticated as "admin:admin123"................................passed When I GET "/api/profile/1".................................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain "alice"................................passed And the response body should not contain "alice123".........................failed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC8 — anonymous browser GET /users redirects to /login
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I open a browser at "http://localhost:8080/users".....................passed Then the current URL should be "/login".....................................failed And the rendered DOM should contain "Sign in"...............................skipped

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC9 — anonymous browser GET /transfer redirects to /login
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I open a browser at "http://localhost:8080/transfer"..................passed Then the current URL should be "/login".....................................failed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC10 — anonymous browser GET /login stays on /login

failure.message:
expected rendered DOM to contain "Sign in" — got: Whitelabel Error Page
This application has no explicit mapping for /error, so you are seeing this as a fallback.
Thu Jul 16 02:10:07 IST 2026 There was an unexpected error (type=Not Found, status=404).

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I open a browser at "http://localhost:8080/login".....................passed Then the current URL should be "/login".....................................passed And the rendered DOM should contain "Sign in"...............................failed

testcase.classname: Authentication enforced globally: anonymous JSON callers get 401 + WWW-Authenticate: Basic, public endpoints stay open
testcase.name: AC11 — post-login browser reaches /users and the DOM has no plaintext password

failure.message: function timed out, ensure the promise resolves within 5000 milliseconds

testcase.failure: at Timeout.<anonymous> (/Users/manishkumar/Workspace/poc2/test/node_modules/@cucumber/cucumber/src/time.ts:52:14) at listOnTimeout (node:internal/timers:594:17) at processTimers (node:internal/timers:529:7)
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I send requests with Accept "application/json"..........................passed Given I open a browser at "http://localhost:8080/login".....................passed When I submit the login form with username "alice" and password "alice123"..failed Then the current URL should be "/dashboard".................................skipped When I navigate the browser to "http://localhost:8080/users"................skipped Then the current URL should be "/users".....................................skipped And the rendered DOM should contain "Users".................................skipped And the rendered DOM should not contain "alice123"..........................skipped And the rendered DOM should not contain "bob123"............................skipped And the rendered DOM should not contain "admin123"..........................skipped

testcase.classname: Unsafe Java deserialization in /api/deserialize is rejected
testcase.name: AC1 — base64 ysoserial-style gadget payload with text/plain is rejected with 415

failure.message: failed to mint CSRF token: GET /dashboard returned 404 — is the app running and the credentials correct?

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated for state-changing requests as "alice:alice123"......failed When I POST "/api/deserialize" as "text/plain" with body:...................skipped Then the response status should be 415......................................skipped And the response body should not contain "java."............................skipped And the response body should not contain "ClassNotFoundException"...........skipped

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/login does not bypass authentication
testcase.name: AC1 — positive control: alice with the correct seeded password returns 200

failure.message: [current response] expected body NOT to contain "password" — got: {"username":"alice","role":"USER","id":1,"password":"alice123"}

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/login does not bypass authentication
testcase.name: AC2 — SQLi tautology in the username field is rejected with 401
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed When I POST "/api/login" with JSON body:....................................passed Then the response status should be 401......................................passed And the response body should not contain "alice"............................passed And the response body should not contain "admin"............................passed And the response body should not contain "bob"..............................passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/login does not bypass authentication
testcase.name: AC3 — UNION-based probe leaks no SQL error and no column-count

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC1 — /api/register hashes the plaintext and never echoes it

failure.message: expected response body NOT to contain the canary password — got: {"id":5,"username":"cipher_test_1784148012645-e7552e74","password":"Sup3rSafe!Passw0rd_1784148012645-e7552e74","email":"cipher_test_1784148012645-e7552e74@example.com","role":"USER","balance":0.0}

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I generate a fresh canary username "cipher_test"........................passed When I POST "/api/register" with the canary credentials.....................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain the canary username....................passed And the response body should not contain the canary password................failed

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC2 — the just-registered canary can log in with the original plaintext (BCrypt verify)

failure.message: [current response] expected body NOT to contain "password" — got: {"username":"cipher_test_1784148012723-73af47a4","role":"USER","id":6,"password":"Sup3rSafe!Passw0rd_1784148012723-73af47a4"}

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I generate a fresh canary username "cipher_test"........................passed When I POST "/api/register" with the canary credentials.....................passed And the response status should be 200.......................................passed When I POST "/api/login" with the canary credentials........................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should contain the canary username....................passed And the response body should contain "role".................................passed And the response body should not contain "password".........................failed

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC3 — login with the wrong password returns 401 (BCrypt verify actually compares)
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I generate a fresh canary username "cipher_test"........................passed When I POST "/api/register" with the canary credentials.....................passed And the response status should be 200.......................................passed When I POST "/api/login" with username "cipher_test" and password "wrong-password-xyz"..passed Then the response status should be 401......................................passed And the response body should not contain the canary username................passed

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC4 — /api/profile/{id} for the canary shows a BCrypt hash or no password field, never the plaintext

failure.message: expected response body NOT to contain the canary password — got: {"id":8,"username":"cipher_test_1784148012750-a69a7807","password":"Sup3rSafe!Passw0rd_1784148012750-a69a7807","email":"cipher_test_1784148012750-a69a7807@example.com","role":"USER","balance":0.0}

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC5 — /api/users as admin shows BCrypt hashes for every row, never the seeded plaintexts

failure.message: [current response] expected body NOT to contain "alice123" — got: [{"id":1,"username":"alice","password":"alice123","email":"alice@example.com","role":"USER","balance":1000.0},{"id":2,"username":"bob","password":"bob123","email":"bob@example.com","role":"USER","balance":500.0},{"id":3,"username":"admin","password":"admin…

testcase.classname: Plain-text password storage: register / login / profile / users endpoints never echo the plaintext
testcase.name: AC6 — a real browser can register a canary and the post-login DOM has no plaintext leak

failure.message: expected response body NOT to contain the canary password — got: {"id":10,"username":"cipher_test_1784148012768-ef2ea58e","password":"Sup3rSafe!Passw0rd_1784148012768-ef2ea58e","email":"cipher_test_1784148012768-ef2ea58e@example.com","role":"USER","balance":0.0}

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/search does not dump the users table
testcase.name: AC1 — exact username search returns the single alice row, with BCrypt-hashed password if any

failure.message: [current response] expected body NOT to contain "alice123" — got: [{"id":1,"username":"alice","password":"alice123","email":"alice@example.com","role":"USER","balance":1000.0}]

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"..................................passed When I GET "/api/search?q=alice"............................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should match a JSON array of length 1.................passed And the response body should contain "alice"................................passed And the response body should not contain "alice123".........................failed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/search does not dump the users table
testcase.name: AC2 — SQLi tautology in q returns zero rows (no mass disclosure)
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"..................................passed When I GET "/api/search?q=' OR '1'='1"......................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should match a JSON array of length 0.................passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/search does not dump the users table
testcase.name: AC3 — UNION probe returns zero rows and leaks no SQL error
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"..................................passed When I GET "/api/search?q=' UNION SELECT 1,2,3,4,5 FROM users--"............passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should match a JSON array of length 0.................passed And the response body should not match "/Hibernate|SQL|ORA-|JpaSystemException|constraint/i"..passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/search does not dump the users table
testcase.name: AC4 — wildcard payload returns zero rows (no LIKE expansion)
testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"..................................passed When I GET "/api/search?q=%25"..............................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should match a JSON array of length 0.................passed

testcase.classname: SQL injection in /api/search does not dump the users table
testcase.name: AC5 — exact username search for bob returns a single row, never a plaintext password

failure.message: [current response] expected body NOT to contain "bob123" — got: [{"id":2,"username":"bob","password":"bob123","email":"bob@example.com","role":"USER","balance":500.0}]

testcase.system-out: Given the vulnerable Spring Boot app is reachable with baseUrl "http://localhost:8080"..passed And I am authenticated as "alice:alice123"..................................passed When I GET "/api/search?q=bob"..............................................passed Then the response status should be 200......................................passed And the response body should match a JSON array of length 1.................passed And the response body should contain "bob"..................................passed And the response body should not contain "bob123"...........................failed
